name: [Tutorial] Getting Started with EEWW
description: Interactive tutorial - learn all features by doing.

steps.title: Welcome & Navigation
steps.instructions:
Welcome to the Emtech EoAT Workbench Wizard tutorial!

This tutorial will walk you through every feature by having you actually use them.

LOOK AROUND THE SCREEN:
• LEFT SIDE: You're reading instructions here. Reference images appear below.
• RIGHT SIDE: Live camera preview. This is where you'll capture images and video.
• TOP: Step counter and navigation buttons (Previous / Next Step).
• BOTTOM: Action buttons for capture, recording, and report generation.

TO PROCEED: You must take a photo to move to the next step. Point your camera at anything and click the 📸 Capture Image button on the right side.

steps.title: Capturing Images
steps.instructions:
Great - you captured your first image!

TIPS:
• You can check your captured images by clicking the "Review Captures" button on the right above the camera preview.
• The step status updates to show the number of photos taken.

You can capture multiple images per step. Each one is saved and will appear in your final report.

TO PROCEED: Capture at least 2 images for this step.

steps.title: Using Annotations
steps.instructions:
Now let's learn the annotation system. Annotations are arrow markers you place on the camera preview to highlight areas of interest.

HOW TO USE ANNOTATIONS:
• LEFT-CLICK on the camera preview to place an arrow marker.
• DRAG a marker to reposition it.
• SCROLL WHEEL over a marker to rotate it.
• SHIFT + SCROLL over a marker to change arrow length (50-300px).
• RIGHT-CLICK a marker to remove it.
• Markers are labeled sequentially (A, B, C...).
• If you delete one, the remaining labels re-order automatically.

TO PROCEED: Place at least one annotation on the preview, then capture an image. The annotation will be permanently embedded in the saved image.

steps.title: Video Recording
steps.instructions:
EEWW can record video with your annotations overlaid in real-time.

HOW TO RECORD:
• Click the 🔴 Start Recording button below the camera preview.
• A red recording indicator with elapsed time appears on the preview.
• Any annotations you place or move during recording are captured in the video.
• Click ⏹ Stop Recording when finished.
• Videos are saved as MP4 (H264) and included in reports.

TRY IT NOW: Start a recording, wait a few seconds, optionally place/move some annotations, then stop recording.

TO PROCEED: Capture a photo (video recording is not enforced as a requirement, but try it!).

steps.title: Pass / Fail Marking
steps.instructions:
Some workflow steps require a Pass or Fail judgment. This step has that requirement enabled.

LOOK FOR:
• The Pass / Fail buttons that appeared for this step.
• Select PASS (green) or FAIL (red) to record your judgment.
• This status is saved with the step and appears in the final report's procedure summary table.

TO PROCEED: Select either Pass or Fail, then capture a photo.

steps.title: Reference Images & Comparison View
steps.instructions:
Workflow steps can include a reference image shown on the left side. This helps technicians compare their work against a known-good example.

FEATURES:
• Reference images appear below the instructions text.
• Use the 🔍 Compare & Overlay button to open a side-by-side comparison window with the live camera feed.
• (Tip- The full size view of the reference image can also be annotated in this view! Click on either the camera view or reference image when fullscreened to add markers and leave notes). If notes are left on the reference image it will also be captured with the "capture image" button.


To proceed to the next step:

steps.title: Creating Overlay Masks
steps.instructions:
EEWW includes a built-in Mask Editor for creating transparent PNG overlays from any image — no external image editing software needed!

HOW TO ACCESS:
• Open the Workflow Editor (from the workflow selection screen, click Edit Workflows).
• Edit any step and click the 🎭 Create Overlay Mask from Image button.

MASK EDITOR FEATURES:
• Load any captured image (JPG, PNG, BMP, etc.) as your starting point.
• Use the Brush, Rectangle, or Ellipse tools to define transparent regions.
• Two paint modes:
  - Paint Transparency (default): Start with the full image visible, paint areas to make see-through.
  - Paint Opacity (inverse): Start fully transparent, paint only the areas you want to keep.
• Left-click paints, right-click erases.
• Scroll wheel to zoom, Ctrl+drag to pan.
• Undo/Redo with Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y (up to 30 levels).
• Toggle the checkerboard preview to see which areas are transparent.
• Saved masks go to resources/overlay_masks/ and can be used as step overlays.

USE CASE: Capture a photo of a correctly assembled component, then mask out everything except alignment guides or key features. The resulting overlay helps technicians align their work during future inspections.

TO PROCEED: Capture a photo.

steps.title: Inspection Checkboxes
steps.instructions:
Workflow steps can include inspection checkboxes overlaid on reference images. This step has example checkboxes.

LOOK AT THE REFERENCE IMAGE BELOW:
• You should see bright amber/yellow checkboxes on the reference image.
• Click each checkbox to mark it as inspected.
• All checkboxes must be checked before you can proceed.
• You can click the reference image to open a full-size resizable view.

NOTE: If no reference image is set for this step, the checkboxes won't appear. Workflow authors add these via the Workflow Editor.

TO PROCEED: Check all inspection checkboxes (if visible) and capture a photo.

steps.title: Camera Overlays (Transparent Masks)
steps.instructions:
PNG OVERLAY MODE:
• If a reference image is a PNG with transparency (alpha channel), it automatically becomes an overlay on the camera feed.
• Use Overlay Settings to adjust: scale, position (X/Y), rotation, and transparency.
• The 'Hide Overlay Image' checkbox temporarily removes the overlay.
• Overlays appear in captured images and video recordings.

TO PROCEED: Capture a photo.

steps.title: Camera Controls
steps.instructions:
You can manage camera settings during a workflow.

CAMERA FEATURES:
• If multiple cameras are connected, use the camera dropdown (top of preview area) to switch between them.
• Click the ⚙️ Camera Settings button to adjust properties like brightness, contrast, exposure, etc.
• If your camera feed looks wrong (e.g., grayscale), use the Restart Camera button in settings to reset.
• Camera discovery is cached at startup for fast loading.

QR CODE SCANNING:
• If pyzbar is installed, the app passively scans for QR codes in the camera feed.
• Detected QR codes are automatically appended to the serial number field.
• No action needed - it runs in the background.

TO PROCEED: Capture a photo.

steps.title: Progress Save & Resume
steps.instructions:
EEWW automatically saves your progress as you work through a workflow.

HOW IT WORKS:
• After completing each step, your progress is auto-saved to output/progress/.
• If you close the app mid-workflow, you can resume from the main menu.
• The Resume button on the mode selection screen shows your saved progress files.
• You can delete individual progress files from the resume dialog.
• Progress files older than 30 days are automatically cleaned up.

ALL captured images, videos, annotations, checkbox states, and pass/fail results are preserved when you resume.

TO PROCEED: Capture a photo.

steps.title: Report Generation
steps.instructions:
When you finish a workflow (or at any time), you can generate a professional report.

REPORT FEATURES:
• Click the 📄 Generate Report button (bottom of screen).
• Choose PDF or DOCX format.
• Reports include: session info, all captured images, videos, annotations, checklist results, and a procedure summary table.
• The summary table shows each step's status: Complete (light green), Pass (green), or Fail (red).
• Reports are saved to output/reports/ and organized by serial number.

THIS IS THE LAST STEP!

TO COMPLETE THE TUTORIAL: Capture a final photo. Then try generating a report to see all your tutorial work compiled into a document.
